Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets start by computing lead times The log is given with timestamps Lets calculate how long each case took from submission to closure

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Total lead time from start 0900 to finish 1030 equals 1 hour and 30 minutes Process is fast with only 90 min total duration

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 start at 0905 on April 1 finish at 1100 on April 2 Total time is around 25 hours and 55 minutes So its long compared to case 2001

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start on April 1 at 0910 finish on April 3 at 0930 total duration around 2 days and 20 min

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 from 0920 to 1045 equals 1 hr and 25 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 start at 0925 on April 1 finish at 1430 on April 4 total about 53 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So the cases with performance issues are clearly those that took longer than expected Cases with high complexity 2003 2005 took much more time than lower complexity ones

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity low Cases 2001 and 2004 process seems efficient Medium complexity case 2002 took about 26 hours High complexity cases 2003 and 2005 took around 48 hours and 53 hours and had multiple requests for additional documents

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Thus the correlation is that claims with higher complexity have more events in Request Additional Documents and longer durations

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The root causes may include High complexity claims require multiple rounds of additional documentation requests which increases lead times Specific resources such as Adjuster Lisa might be handling many cases with delays or Manager Bill perhaps takes longer in certain cases The process in region B appears to have a delay in the additional documents step if claim is medium or high complexity

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Region A Cases 2001 and 2003 one low complexity fast and one high complexity slow Region B Cases 2002 2004 2005 low complexity fast but medium and high complexity slower

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: It might be that high complexity claims require additional back and forth which increases lead time Also the location of requests for additional docs and resource performance might vary by region if certain regions have slower response times or more bureaucracy

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Propose explanations The extended durations appear to be strongly correlated with higher claim complexities as they require multiple rounds of documentation review Geographic region B might have inherent inefficiencies in processing claims because medium and high complexity cases are slower there Some resources may be overloaded or not working at optimum speed

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Suggestions Standardize process steps and reduce variation by creating guidelines on when additional documents should be requested especially for high complexity claims Provide specialized training for resources handling complex claims Implement automation where possible Monitor resource performance and consider workload balancing
